channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Baguio and Other Remote Areas Experience Increased Fuel Costs Due to Logistics
item.description: Diesel prices in Baguio City have climbed to around P90 per liter, significantly higher than in other parts of the country, largely because of the city's distance from the nearest oil depot in Poro Po...

item.title: Transporters raise concerns over new port tariffs and impact on trade costs in Tanzania
item.description: Transporters and traders in Tanzania are expressing serious concerns about the new domestic cargo charges introduced by the Tanzania Ports Authority (TPA), which started on March 8, 2025. They warn th...

item.title: UK Royal Navy Faces Deployment Delays for Cyprus Base Protection Amid Limited Warship Availability
item.description: The United Kingdom is struggling to respond effectively to threats against its sovereign military bases in Cyprus, particularly RAF Akrotiri, following a drone attack linked to escalating regional con...

item.title: Pakistani Courts Implement Four-Day Work Week Amid Fuel Crisis
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan’s courts are now moving to a four-day work week from Monday to Thursday as part of austerity measures due to the ongoing fuel crisis triggered by the Middle East conflict. The Supr...

item.title: Meta Acquires Moltbook to Integrate AI Agent Social Platform into Superintelligence Labs
item.description: Meta has announced its acquisition of Moltbook, a social network designed primarily for AI agents, though humans can observe interactions. Founded by Matt Schlicht and Ben Parr, Moltbook gained attent...

item.title: Biodun Otunola becomes Mexico’s Honorary Consul in Lagos to boost trade and cooperation
item.description: On Monday, Lagos dey witness important diplomatic waka as prominent Nigerian businessman and infrastructure developer, Biodun Otunola, don officially become the Honorary Consul of Mexico for Lagos. Na...

item.title: US warns Iran over Strait of Hormuz oil flow amid escalating West Asia conflict
item.description: The United States has issued a strong warning to Iran over the Strait of Hormuz, one of the most strategic maritime routes in the world. President Donald Trump stated that if Iran stops the flow of oi...

item.title: David Littleproud steps down as Nationals leader amid party turbulence
item.description: David Littleproud has unexpectedly resigned as leader of the Nationals, citing exhaustion and stating he is 'out on his feet.' His departure follows a turbulent period for the party and the Coalition,...

item.title: Trump's granddaughter sparks debate with lavish shopping video amid global tensions
item.description: The Guardian columnist Marina Hyde critiques a video posted by Donald Trump’s eldest granddaughter, Kai Trump, in which she visits the ultra-expensive Los Angeles grocery store Erewhon. The 18-year-ol...

item.title: How Scandinavian Cities Make Winter Cycling Safe and Practical
item.description: In Scandinavia, cycling remains a common mode of transport during winter, despite cold temperatures and snow. Cities like Stockholm, Oslo, and Copenhagen see significant numbers of people commuting by...

item.title: Oil Demand Shifts from Fuel to Industrial Materials as Transport Moves to Electrification
item.description: While global oil consumption remains high, the rise of electric vehicles is expected to plateau demand for oil as a fuel. Transport, which has historically been the largest consumer of crude oil, is g...

item.title: Supreme Court to decide on family’s plea for withdrawal of life support for permanently vegetative patient
item.description: The Supreme Court of India is set to deliver its judgment on Wednesday, 11th March 2026, regarding a plea filed by the family of Harish Rana, a 31-year-old man who has been in a permanent vegetative s...

item.title: No jackpot winners in PCSO major lotto draws on March 10
item.description: On March 10, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported that no one won the jackpot prizes in the major lotto draws. For the Lotto 6/42, the winning numbers 03-29-12-33-10-16 were...

item.title: Gauteng launches specialised unit to recover outstanding vehicle licence fees
item.description: The Gauteng government has announced the establishment of a dedicated debt collection unit to pursue motorists who owe millions in outstanding vehicle licence fees. Gauteng MEC for Finance and Economi...

item.title: Government and private schools join forces to advance Education Policy 2014
item.description: The Ministry of Education, Science and Technology in Tanzania has been strengthening its collaboration with private schools to support the implementation of the Education and Training Policy 2014. Thi...

item.title: Former Rivers Deputy Governor Harry-Banigo Joins APC Amid PDP Crisis
item.description: Senator Ipalibo Harry-Banigo wey be former deputy governor for Rivers State don officially waka from Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) enter All Progressives Congress (APC). She yan dis one for a letter ...

item.title: Melkbosstrand Beach Closed for Third Time in Three Weeks Due to Separate Sewage Incidents
item.description: Melkbosstrand Beach, a popular West Coast spot near Cape Town, has faced repeated temporary closures over the past three weeks due to sewage-related problems, leaving local residents increasingly frus...

item.title: ICC Fines Arshdeep Singh for Ball-Throwing Incident During T20 World Cup Final
item.description: In the Twenty20 World Cup final, Indian fast bowler Arshdeep Singh was penalised by the International Cricket Council (ICC) after he threw the ball at New Zealand batsman Daryl Mitchell. The incident ...

item.title: UNILAG Lecturers Begin Indefinite Strike Over Partial Payment of January and February 2026 Salaries
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) at the University of Lagos don declare say dem go enter indefinite strike from Wednesday, March 11, 2026, because their January and February salaries no...

item.title: Madagascar President reshuffles government after seizing power
item.description: Madagascar's President Michael Randrianirina, who came into power in October following youth-led protests that ousted his predecessor, has dismissed the country's prime minister and the entire cabinet...
